name: Canvas2D Space Scroller
description: Reusable, config-driven space cave-flyer: pilot a ship with inertial thrust through a scrolling tilemap level with real per-axis wall collision, over multi-layer parallax starfields. Solid tiles block the ship, hazard tiles destroy it (lives), collectibles add score, reach the exit to clear the level. Procedural fallback graphics; standalone browser export.
capabilities: multi-layer parallax starfields (and color tint layers) scrolled by the camera
tilemap level ('#' solid wall, hazard char, else empty) with a scrolling camera that follows the ship
inertial ship thrust physics with damping and a speed cap
real per-axis AABB wall collision (walls block; the ship cannot pass through)
hazard tiles crash the ship (lives + respawn invulnerability)
collectibles (score) and level-complete on reaching the exit (optionally requires all collectibles)
standalone export (relative paths, no server calls)
constraints: vanilla HTML/CSS/JS only — no Node, npm or frameworks
configuration-driven: generators produce JSON matching schema.json, never runtime code
relative asset paths only; missing sprites fall back to procedural drawings
no shooting in v1 — this is a navigation/flight template; the map is JSON-defined (no procedural generation)
